assertion_results.text: Version impact table shows mixed results: 2.1.x versions marked YES (affected) and 2.2.x versions marked NO (not affected) based on lock file evidence
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md lines 69-77: 2.1.0 = YES (h2 0.4.5), 2.1.1 = YES (h2 0.4.5), 2.2.0 = NO (h2 0.4.8), 2.2.1 = NO (h2 0.4.8), 2.2.2 = NO (retag), 2.2.3 = NO (h2 0.4.9), 2.2.4 = NO (h2 0.4.9). Line 79: 'Stream 2.1.x is AFFECTED...Stream 2.2.x is NOT AFFECTED'

assertion_results.text: Affects Versions correction includes only the actually affected versions (2.1.x versions), not the unaffected 2.2.x versions (Step 3.2)
assertion_results.evidence: affects-versions.md line 48: 'Proposed Affects Versions: [RHTPA 2.1.0, RHTPA 2.1.1]' and lines 55-56: 'REMOVE: RHTPA 2.2.0 (not affected)' and 'ADD: RHTPA 2.1.1 (affected)'

assertion_results.text: Remediation tasks are created only for the affected stream (2.1.x), not for 2.2.x which already ships the patched version (Step 8 Case A scoping)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md line 16: 'Remediation tasks are created **only for stream 2.1.x** (the affected stream). Stream 2.2.x already ships the patched version and requires no remediation.'

assertion_results.text: The analysis treats the issue as unscoped (no stream suffix) and checks ALL versions across all streams (Step 1 Stream scope resolution, unscoped path)
assertion_results.evidence: data-extraction.md line 66: 'this issue is treated as **unscoped**. Per the skill protocol, unscoped issues cover all streams. Steps 2-8 will analyze ALL versions across ALL configured streams (2.1.x and 2.2.x).' version-impact.md line 24: 'All versions across both streams are included'

assertion_results.text: Cross-stream impact notice is NOT generated because the issue is unscoped — it covers all streams by definition (Step 8 Case B applies only to scoped issues)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md lines 12-13: 'Case B does NOT apply -- the issue is unscoped, so cross-stream impact notice is not generated. Cross-stream notices apply only to scoped issues where other streams outside the issue's scope are also affected. Since this issue covers all streams, there is no "outside scope."'
